title: PCB Project Requirements Intake
objective: Convert an unstructured device description into a verifiable PCB Design Brief, an initial constraint set, and explicit open questions.
inputs: power rails, interfaces, frequencies, currents, and voltages
size, cost, manufacturing, environment, and certification constraints
workflow: assign each requirement an ID, priority, source, and verification method
derive electrical, mechanical, manufacturing, and test constraints
prepare a traceable handoff to downstream skills
runtime: Analyze the device requirements <description>; produce a PCB Design Brief, constraints, risks, and open questions without changing the design.

title: System Architecture and Functional Partitioning
objective: Define the hardware architecture, signal flow, power flow, and functional-domain boundaries before detailed schematic capture.
workflow: partition the device into functional blocks
identify analog, digital, RF, high-current, and safety domains
assign preliminary net criticality and classes
acceptance: every interface has defined levels and direction
every rail has identified sources and consumers
domain boundaries have an engineering rationale
runtime: Use the Design Brief to define hardware architecture, the power tree, interfaces, and critical nets without editing the project.

title: PCB Constraint Manager
objective: Normalize requirements into one versioned set of ERC, DRC, net-class, differential-pair, length, mechanical, and manufacturing constraints.
workflow: identify gaps, conflicts, and infeasible constraints
prepare a semantic dry-run of rule updates
runtime: Normalize the project constraints, compare them with current DipTrace rules, and prepare a dry-run correction plan.

title: End-to-End PCB Design Orchestrator
objective: Coordinate specialized skills, dependency sequencing, and quality gates from requirements through release.
workflow: prevent unresolved critical findings from being bypassed
acceptance: there are no hidden write operations
every task has an explicit completion criterion
a resumed run starts from verified current state
runtime: Assess the project state and plan the required PCB skills through release without applying changes.

title: Component Selection and Preliminary BOM
maturity: full automation requires a sourcing adapter
objective: Select components and alternatives against requirements, ratings, package, lifecycle, availability, and cost.
workflow: derive candidates only from verified sources
record approved alternatives and library requirements
acceptance: every selection traces to a requirement
pin compatibility is never claimed without verification
runtime: Select components for <block>, compare alternatives, and produce preliminary BOM candidates with risks without changing the schematic.

title: Library Symbol and Footprint Creation
objective: Create or update DipTrace component and pattern items from verified datasheets and package drawings.
workflow: extract pins and dimensions with provenance
prepare the symbol and electrical pin types
prepare pattern, mask, paste, assembly, and silkscreen geometry
acceptance: silkscreen does not overlap mask openings
runtime: Create a symbol and footprint for <MPN> from its datasheet; show extracted evidence and previews before any write.

title: Library Quality Audit
objective: Audit symbols, patterns, pin mapping, mask and paste, origins, markings, duplicates, and library revisions.
workflow: verify pads, drills, annular rings, mask, paste, origin, and courtyard
prepare repair transactions without silent remapping
acceptance: pin mapping is never changed implicitly
runtime: Audit libraries in <scope>, identify defects, and prepare guarded repair plans.

title: Schematic Authoring
objective: Build a readable schematic incrementally from the architecture while continuously validating connectivity and ERC.
workflow: partition the schematic into sheets and blocks
create wires, labels, buses, and hierarchy
run localized ERC after each block
acceptance: there are no accidental unnamed nets
runtime: Implement schematic block <name>; produce a plan and preview, run ERC, and commit only after explicit confirmation.

title: Schematic ERC and Engineering Review
objective: Combine deterministic ERC with explainable engineering heuristics while keeping their evidence classes distinct.
workflow: check power sources, inputs, and output conflicts
check floating inputs, labels, hierarchy, and patterns
acceptance: findings include object references and rationale
ambiguous behavior is never corrected automatically
runtime: Run full ERC and an engineering review of the active schematic without changing it.

title: Power Tree Review
objective: Build a directed power graph and review budgets, ratings, sequencing, protection, and reverse-current paths.
acceptance: a pass is never declared without required data
runtime: Build and review the project power tree, calculate rail budgets, and derive layout constraints.

title: Decoupling Network Planner
objective: Define local, group, and bulk capacitors and verify their actual connectivity and placement relative to power pins.
workflow: assign a role to every capacitor
verify rating, dielectric, package, and assumptions
acceptance: every capacitor has an explicit function
runtime: Review decoupling for <IC/rail>, produce a capacitor-to-pin plan, and assess physical placement.

title: Reset, Boot, and Programming Review
objective: Review reset circuits, strap pins, boot modes, programming, debug, and recovery access.
workflow: identify reset, boot, and debug pins
check pulls, timing, and voltage domains
acceptance: debug access does not conflict with the application
runtime: Review reset, boot straps, and programming/debug access for <MCU/SoC> in the schematic and PCB.

title: Interface Compliance Review
objective: Review USB, Ethernet, HDMI, CAN, RS-485, UART, I2C, SPI, PCIe, MIPI, and other interfaces against parameterized profiles.
inputs: interface type, revision, and data rate
workflow: check pinout, levels, terminations, pulls, ESD, and common-mode provisions
check routing, impedance, length, and return path
identify solver and certification checks that remain external
acceptance: the checklist matches the stated standard revision
heuristic evidence is not presented as certification
data rate and assumptions are explicit
runtime: Review interface <type/revision> across the schematic and PCB, including protection, routing, and return path.

title: Schematic-to-PCB Consistency
objective: Compare RefDes, nets, values, patterns, fields, and pin-to-pad mapping across schematic, PCB, and libraries.
workflow: prepare a transactional ECO synchronization plan
runtime: Compare the schematic and PCB for RefDes, nets, values, patterns, and pin-to-pad mapping; prepare an ECO without committing.

title: Stackup and Impedance Planner
objective: Select stackup alternatives, reference planes, and preliminary controlled-impedance trace geometry.
workflow: assign signal and reference layer pairs
prepare rule updates and fabricator questions
acceptance: unknown dielectric constants and thicknesses are not substituted
analytical estimates and solver-backed results remain distinct
runtime: Plan a stackup for <requirements>, calculate widths and gaps, and prepare rule updates without committing.

title: Board Outline and Mechanical Constraints
maturity: requires geometry and board-structure write support
objective: Create and validate board outlines, cutouts, holes, keepouts, and the mechanical envelope.
workflow: define the datum and coordinate system
acceptance: critical dimensions appear in the preview
runtime: Validate or plan the board outline from mechanical data <description>, including keepouts and a preview.

title: Functional Block Placement
objective: Perform floorplanning from signal flow, power flow, criticality, thermal constraints, and routing corridors.
workflow: score wirelength, crossings, density, and congestion
acceptance: no new overlap or edge violation is introduced
runtime: Plan floorplacement for block <RefDes> in region <region>; show ranked variants and score breakdowns.

title: Connector and Mechanical Placement
maturity: requires geometry and placement write support
objective: Place connectors, controls, LEDs, antennas, and sensors against enclosure datums and mating constraints.
acceptance: objects are locked only after preview and confirmation
runtime: Place connectors <RefDes> at the supplied mechanical coordinates and verify access and routing corridors.

title: Power Stage Placement
objective: Minimize high-current loops, switching-node area, feedback coupling, and thermal risk.
workflow: place switches, inductors, diodes, and capacitors
isolate switching nodes from feedback paths
acceptance: feedback paths are protected from noisy nodes
runtime: Optimize placement of power stage <RefDes/nets>, minimize loop proxies, and derive routing constraints.

title: High-Speed Component Placement
objective: Place PHYs, clocks, memory, connectors, terminations, and protection according to topology and routing needs.
acceptance: terminations are on the topology-correct side
runtime: Optimize placement for interface <type/nets>, including the PHY, connector, ESD, and termination components.

title: Analog and RF Placement
objective: Plan low-noise analog and RF placement, grounding, matching networks, antenna keepouts, and isolation.
workflow: define domains without breaking return paths
acceptance: separation does not break return paths
runtime: Review and optimize placement for analog/RF block <description>.

title: Placement Optimizer and Legalizer
objective: Improve placement incrementally within a bounded scope without discarding engineering intent.
runtime: Optimize placement <selector> in <region> using weights <parameters>; show three ranked variants.

title: Silkscreen Cleanup
objective: Place RefDes, value, polarity, and custom labels without collisions or loss of readability.
workflow: score collision, readability, and component association
acceptance: no new silkscreen violation is introduced
runtime: Clean silkscreen on <Top/Bottom/all>, preserve locked labels, and show a preview.

title: Testpoint Planner
objective: Select nets and place testpoints against coverage targets and fixture geometry.
workflow: cover power, ground, and programming interfaces
runtime: Plan testpoints for <coverage> on side <side> with probe constraints <parameters>.

title: Routing Priority Planner
objective: Determine routing sequence, batches, and corridors from criticality, topology, and congestion.
workflow: identify escape routing, clocks, differential pairs, and power
acceptance: the ordering has an engineering rationale
runtime: Plan the routing order for the active board and group nets into batches without routing them.

title: Critical Net Router
objective: Generate and compare bounded route plans for individual critical nets.
workflow: score length, bends, vias, and return path
stage the selected plan and run localized validation
acceptance: there are no shorts or regressions
runtime: Plan a route for critical net <net> within <region/layers>; show alternatives without committing.

title: Power Routing
maturity: requires routing and thermal analysis support
objective: Plan power routes, via arrays, and copper areas against current, voltage-drop, and thermal targets.
workflow: check thermal relief and return paths
runtime: Plan power routing for rail <net> at <A> with <drop/temp> targets; start with a dry-run.

title: Differential Pair Router
objective: Route, measure, and tune differential pairs while controlling impedance, skew, and symmetry.
workflow: measure coupled length, skew, and via symmetry
acceptance: positive and negative nets are mapped correctly
gap and skew are measured along the route
transitions are symmetric or explicitly justified
runtime:
Plan a differential-pair route for

/ under constraints ; produce a plan and preview first.

title: Bus Length Matching
objective: Route and tune related nets using source-synchronous topology, skew budgets, and bounded tuning regions.
workflow: check crosstalk risk and final deltas
acceptance: meanders do not create excessive coupling
length and skew deltas are measured
runtime: Review and match lengths for group <nets> within <tolerance>; show the tuning plan.

title: External Autorouter Orchestrator
objective: Run a guarded DSN-to-autorouter-to-SES workflow and compare the result with the routing baseline.
workflow: compare DRC, connectivity, vias, and lengths
apply only an explicitly accepted result
acceptance: execution is limited to the adapter whitelist
the source design remains unchanged before preview
runtime: Run Freerouting for <scope> with budget <time>; import only into preview and compare metrics.

title: Route Cleanup and Legalization
objective: Reduce unnecessary bends, vias, and detours and legalize geometry without changing connectivity.
workflow: find unnecessary bends, vias, neck-downs, and acute angles
compare length, via count, and return path
runtime: Clean routes in <nets/region>, reducing unnecessary vias and bends without changing connectivity.

title: Copper Pour and Plane Manager
objective: Analyze pours and planes for priorities, islands, necks, thermal relief, and continuity.
acceptance: an approximate refill is never called authoritative
runtime: Review pours and planes, identify islands and necks, and prepare a dry-run change plan.

title: Return and Stitching Via Planner
objective: Plan return vias at transitions and stitching or fence vias against reference, EMC, and RF constraints.
acceptance: each via is assigned to the correct net
runtime: Find candidate return and stitching vias for <region/nets> and prepare a guarded plan.

title: DRC and Connectivity Review
objective: Run, group, and prioritize geometry and connectivity findings and regressions.
workflow: separate new, existing, and waived findings
acceptance: rule provenance and measured delta are present
runtime: Run full DRC and connectivity review, compare it with the baseline, and make no changes.

title: Controlled Impedance Review
objective: Review stackup, widths, gaps, reference layers, neck-downs, and transitions for controlled-impedance nets.
workflow: identify solver and fabricator confirmation requirements
acceptance: the model and method are stated
analytical and solver results remain distinct
runtime: Run a controlled-impedance review for <nets/classes> against the current stackup.

title: Signal Integrity Review
objective: Perform an explainable SI review of topology, impedance, terminations, stubs, crosstalk, and return paths.
acceptance: an eye-diagram pass is never claimed without simulation
runtime: Run an SI review of critical nets <list>, including stubs, crosstalk risk, and return paths.

title: Power Integrity Review
objective: Review the PDN from rail budgets and decoupling through plane necks, vias, and high-current loops.
acceptance: loads and operating modes are stated
heuristics are not presented as full simulation
each bottleneck is linked to a rail
runtime: Run a PI/PDN review of rails <nets>, including decoupling, planes, and vias.

title: Return Path Review
objective: Review reference-plane continuity, splits, voids, transitions, and return-via coverage.
acceptance: references are derived from the stackup
recommendations do not create plane necks
runtime: Run a return-path review for <nets/classes>, identifying split crossings and poor transitions.

title: EMC/EMI Pre-Compliance Review
objective: Identify emission and immunity risk mechanisms in the schematic and layout before laboratory testing.
workflow: identify high dV/dt and dI/dt sources
check edge routing, plane gaps, and common-mode paths
acceptance: cable and enclosure assumptions are stated
runtime: Run an EMC/EMI pre-compliance review focused on <standard/subsystems>.

title: Thermal Review
objective: Evaluate heat sources, copper spreading, thermal vias, clustering, and current-heating bottlenecks.
workflow: identify heat sources and temperature-sensitive parts
acceptance: results without CFD are marked heuristic
runtime: Run a thermal review of <RefDes/region> under conditions <description>.

title: Design for Manufacturing Review
objective: Review the design and available outputs against one versioned PCB fabricator profile.
inputs: design plus Gerber and Excellon when available
workflow: check trace, spacing, drill, annular ring, and edge constraints
acceptance: the profile has an immutable version
rules from different fabricators are not mixed
runtime: Run a DFM review against fabricator profile <fabricator> and identify release blockers.

title: Design for Assembly Review
objective: Review spacing, access, polarity, orientation, paste, fiducials, and assembly-process risks.
workflow: check fiducials, tooling, and assembly notes
acceptance: DNP state and variants are included
runtime: Run a DFA review for process <SMT/THT/mixed> and assembler profile <assembler>.

title: Design for Test Review
maturity: requires testpoint and testability review support
objective: Review test coverage, programming and debug access, probe geometry, and production-test feasibility.
workflow: measure coverage with an explicit denominator
acceptance: inaccessible points are not counted as covered
production and debug coverage remain distinct
runtime: Run a DFT review for strategy <description> and prepare a plan for missing testpoints.

title: BOM Review
objective: Review BOM completeness, consistency, variants, DNP state, MPNs, and package mapping.
workflow: compare the BOM with the design
check value, package, and MPN consistency
evaluate sourcing risk only through an explicit adapter
runtime: Review the BOM for required fields, variants, DNP state, and package/MPN consistency.

title: Fabrication Package Generator
objective: Generate, verify, and package fabrication outputs reproducibly through a confirmed native exporter.
workflow: export layers, drills, and drawings only through a confirmed adapter
verify the layer set and drills
create a manifest and file hashes
verify with an independent parser or renderer
acceptance: every file has a hash and purpose
no stale revision file is included
runtime: Generate and verify a fabrication package for profile <fabricator>, including a complete manifest.

title: Assembly Package Generator
objective: Generate BOM, CPL or pick-and-place, assembly drawings, and variant instructions with verified transforms.
workflow: export CPL only through a confirmed adapter
verify sample coordinates, sides, and rotations
acceptance: top and bottom transforms are verified
runtime: Generate an assembly package for variant <name> and assembler profile <assembler>, including CPL verification.

title: Panelization
maturity: requires panelization support or an adapter
objective: Plan panel arrangements, rails, tabs or V-score, fiducials, tooling, and breakaway features.
runtime: Plan a panel for <size/method/fabricator>, showing ranked arrangements and utilization.

title: Final Release Gate
maturity: depends on review and export skills
objective: Issue a formal pass or blocked decision for a revision from mandatory reviews, waivers, and output manifests.
acceptance: PASS is forbidden when a mandatory check is unknown
runtime: Run the release gate for the current revision under profile <profile> without fixing anything.

title: ECO Change Impact Analysis
objective: Formalize a change request, derive its impact graph, and prepare a guarded semantic transaction.
workflow: find impacted objects, rules, and outputs
apply only after preview and checks
runtime: Prepare an ECO for <change>, showing its impact graph and semantic plan without committing.

title: Semantic Design Diff and Regression Review
objective: Compare revisions by normalized domain model rather than XML text and identify engineering regressions.
workflow: compare components, nets, rules, placement, routes, and stackup
acceptance: XML reordering is not treated as a design change
object identity mapping is stable or ambiguity is reported
runtime: Compare revisions <base> and <candidate>, producing a semantic diff and regression report.

title: Board Bring-Up Plan
objective: Produce a safe, staged bring-up procedure with expected measurements and stop conditions.
acceptance: every step has an expected result
every hazardous step has a stop condition
runtime: Create a bring-up plan for the current board using lab equipment <list>.

title: Manufacturing Test Plan
objective: Convert DFT requirements into ICT, FCT, and programming workflows, coverage, and a fixture map.
workflow: define the fault model and coverage
map tests to probes and connectors
acceptance: every test traces to a requirement or fault
runtime: Develop a production test plan for volume <N> and fixture capabilities <capabilities>.

title: Failure Analysis and Rework Planning
objective: Rank failure hypotheses from symptoms and design evidence, select diagnostic measurements, and prepare rework or an ECO.
workflow: correlate power, connectivity, and layout evidence
prepare an ECO only after root cause is supported
acceptance: hypotheses are not presented as facts
runtime: Analyze failure <symptoms/measurements>, rank hypotheses, and produce diagnostic and rework plans.

title: Design Documentation Generator
objective: Generate revision-linked documentation from the actual schematic, PCB, constraints, BOM, and reports.
workflow: bind outputs to document hash and revision
acceptance: there are no unsupported numeric claims
generated and manual sections are separated
runtime: Generate documentation for the current revision covering architecture, power, interfaces, stackup, BOM, bring-up, and open issues.

title: Reverse-Engineering Audit
objective: Reconstruct architecture, functional blocks, power tree, and critical paths without inventing design intent.
acceptance: inferred intent is separated from connectivity facts
runtime: Audit the existing design and reconstruct architecture, power tree, interfaces, and explicit unknowns.

title: Multi-Agent PCB Coordinator
objective: Delegate PCB work packages to specialized agents while controlling scopes, conflicts, and independent review.
workflow: split the goal into work packages
acceptance: parallel write scopes do not conflict
runtime: Partition task <goal> among PCB agents and assign scopes and quality gates.

title: PCB Skill Evaluator
objective: Evaluate accuracy, safety, graceful degradation, determinism, and regression behavior of other PCB skills.
workflow: validate package structure and capability contracts
run fixtures in an isolated scope
produce a scorecard and remediation tasks
acceptance: a skill is never marked ready without acceptance evidence
false success is a critical failure
runtime: Evaluate skill <slug> against fixture suite <suite> for accuracy, safety, determinism, and regression behavior.
